title: package.json exposes no test or standalone typecheck entrypoint for a TypeScript Next.js app
reasoning: The only verification script declared is `lint`. In this TypeScript/Next.js project, there is no `test` script and no standalone `typecheck` script, so CI or release automation driven from `package.json` cannot explicitly run behavioral tests or a dedicated type-check step. That makes regressions easier to ship and forces tooling to rely on framework-specific side effects of other commands instead of a stable contract.
recommendation: Add explicit verification scripts in `package.json`, for example a `typecheck` script using `tsc --noEmit` and a `test` script once the project test runner is chosen. Wire release/CI flows to run those scripts alongside lint.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests were provided, and the `scripts` block does not define a `test` command or any standalone typecheck command.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add CI coverage that runs `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm test` on every change, failing the build if any command is missing or exits non-zero.
